s3_raw.text: Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new service, or platform, to provide free cancer medicines to thousands of children in less wealthy nations. The WHO defines these as low- and middle-income countries. The first medicines were to be provided to Mongolia and Uzbekistan. The next shipments would go to Ecuador, Jordan, Nepal and Zambia, as part of the project's testing, or pilot period, the WHO said. The drug treatments are expected to reach around 5,000 children with cancer this year across at least 30 hospitals in the six countries. "Countries in the pilot phase will receive an uninterrupted supply of quality-assured childhood cancer medicines at no cost," the U.N. health agency said in a statement. The WHO said childhood cancer survival rates in low- and middle-income countries were often below 30 percent.
16. What is the main purpose of the new WHO service?
A. To sell cancer medicines to wealthy countries.
B. To build thirty new cancer hospitals.
C. To provide free cancer drugs to children in less wealthy nations.
D. To find new doctors to work in Uzbekistan and Mongolia.
17. Which countries were the first to receive medicines from this program?
A. Ecuador and Jordan
B. Mongolia and Uzbekistan
C. Nepal and Zambia
D. Ecuador and Nepal
18. How many children with cancer are expected to receive treatments during this year?
A. Around 5,000 children
B. Exactly 30 children
C. Around 30,000 children
D. Fewer than 500 children
19. What does the WHO promise to countries in the pilot phase?
A. A continuous supply of high-quality cancer medicines at no cost.
B. A team of doctors who will visit the countries every month.
C. Free medical equipment for thirty of their hospitals.
D. Money to build more childhood cancer centers.
20. According to the passage, what is the survival rate for childhood cancer in low- and middle-income nations?
A. It is usually around 50 percent.
B. It is often lower than 30 percent.
C. It is higher than in wealthy countries.
D. It has already reached 100 percent.
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

French scientists have announced they set a new record of 22 minutes for keeping hydrogen atoms in a state called plasma. The team said their work marks an important step in the search for nuclear fusion methods that could produce massive amounts of clean energy in the future. France’s Atomic Energy Commission (CEA) said the experiment – carried out February 12 – showed the process kept, or maintained, a state of plasma for 1,337 seconds. The CEA added that the new record was 25 percent longer than the previous one, set in China last month. The aim of fusion research is to create the same kind of nuclear reaction that happens in the center of the sun. In a fusion reaction, the centers of two atoms fuse, or join together, to form a new atom with a heavier nucleus, or center.
16. How long did the French scientists keep the hydrogen atoms in a plasma state during the experiment?
A. 12 minutes
B. 22 minutes
C. 25 minutes
D. 37 minutes
17. Where was the previous record for maintaining the plasma state set?
A. In France
B. In the sun
C. In China
D. In the United States
18. What is the main goal of nuclear fusion research?
A. To create a massive amount of clean energy
B. To find a way to travel to the sun
C. To decrease the weight of atoms
D. To change the temperature of the Earth
19. What happens to the two atoms during a fusion reaction?
A. They split into smaller parts.
B. They join together to form a new atom.
C. They are completely destroyed.
D. They turn into cold liquid.
20. According to the passage, France's new record is ________ than the previous record.
A. 12 percent longer
B. 22 percent longer
C. 25 percent longer
D. 50 percent longer
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Yellowstone National Park in the western United States was the world’s first national park. Established in 1872, it is famous for its geothermal activity and wild animals. It includes beautiful mountains, deep canyons, lakes and rivers. Yellowstone is still one of the largest national parks in the U.S. It takes up almost 9,000 square kilometers. Most of the park lies within the western state of Wyoming. The park is unusual because of what lies underneath it. It sits on top of an ancient super volcano, known as the Yellowstone Caldera. The caldera remains an active volcano. A lake of hot liquid rock is about six kilometers under the park. Experts say this lake is under huge amounts of pressure. The pressure and heat cause geysers to shoot hot water out of the ground and mud to boil at Yellowstone.
16. When was Yellowstone National Park established?
17. In which American state is most of the park located?
A. Montana
B. Wyoming
C. Idaho
D. California
18. According to the passage, why is Yellowstone National Park unusual?
A. It has no mountains or deep canyons.
B. It is the smallest national park in the world.
C. It sits on top of an active super volcano.
D. It does not have any wild animals.
19. How deep under the park is the lake of hot liquid rock?
A. About three kilometers
B. About six kilometers
C. About nine kilometers
D. About twelve kilometers
20. What causes the geysers at Yellowstone to shoot hot water out of the ground?
A. The cold winter weather
B. Deep lakes and rivers
C. Pressure and heat from the underground lake
D. The large size of the park
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

In Senegal, educators are starting to teach English to younger children in an effort to better prepare them to succeed in future educational and professional activities. The preschool students also learn French – the country’s official language – as well as Arabic and the nation’s national languages, the French news agency AFP reports. Senegalese children who attend preschool – also known as nursery school, are generally under five. Since mid-January, the education ministry has been testing a program that teaches English in more than 600 nursery and primary school classes. It is part of a plan to better connect Senegal with the rest of the world. At one nursery school visited by AFP reporters near central Dakar, five-year-old students were heard singing, "Good morning, teacher!" The teacher, Absa Ndiaye, said, "They're interested in the lesson, and they start a conversation with 'How are you?'" Senegal recently became an oil and gas producer and has seen large population growth.
16. Why did educators in Senegal decide to start teaching English to young children?
A. To help them do well in their future studies and jobs.
B. To replace French as the country's official language.
C. To make them forget their national languages.
D. To prepare them to move to another country immediately.
17. Which language is the official language of Senegal that preschool children also learn?
A. Arabic
B. English
C. French
D. Spanish
18. How many nursery and primary school classes are testing the new English program?
A. Fewer than 100 classes
B. More than 600 classes
C. Exactly 50 classes
D. Around 5 classes
19. What is one main goal of teaching English in Senegal according to the passage?
A. To stop using national languages in public areas.
B. To better connect the country with the rest of the world.
C. To increase the country's population growth.
D. To help the country produce more oil and gas.
20. According to the teacher, Absa Ndiaye, how do the children feel about their English lessons?
A. They are bored and do not want to talk.
B. They find the language too difficult to learn.
C. They only want to speak in French.
D. They are interested and start simple conversations.
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

In 2024, the number of babies born in South Korea increased for the first time in nine years. The change is welcome news for a country that is dealing with serious population problems. South Korea’s statistics agency said recently that 238,300 babies were born last year, an increase of 8,300 from a year earlier. The agency said the country’s fertility rate — the average number of babies born to each woman in her reproductive years — was 0.75 in 2024, up from 0.72 in 2023. The data represents the first time that the yearly number of births has increased since 2015. Choi Yoon Kyung is an expert with the Korea Institute of Child Care and Education. Choi told the Associated Press that researchers must wait for more data over the next few years to see if increased births were driven by “structural changes.” Park Hyun Jung is with the government agency Statistics Korea.
16. What is the main topic of the passage?
A. The career of a South Korean population expert
B. The recent rise in the number of births in South Korea
C. The changes in South Korea's childcare system
D. How South Korea's statistics agency collects information
17. How many babies were born in South Korea in 2024?
18. What was the fertility rate in South Korea in 2023?
19. Before 2024, when was the last time the number of yearly births increased in South Korea?
20. According to expert Choi Yoon Kyung, what do researchers need to do next?
A. Ask the government for more money
B. Wait for more data in the coming years
C. Stop collecting birth statistics
D. Build more child care centers
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

New research suggests the risk of Americans developing dementia over a lifetime may be much higher than once thought. A new study predicts about one million Americans will likely develop the disease each year by 2060. That is about twice the number of people estimated in earlier studies. The new finding is based on a large study involving a team from major U.S. medical centers and other research organizations. It recently appeared in the publication Nature Medicine. The study found that after age 55, people face up to a 42 percent chance of developing dementia, if they live long enough. The research showed the risk of dementia is highest after age 75. But the good news is that there are steps people can take to reduce the risks. One of them is controlling high blood pressure and other conditions, such as diabetes or being severely overweight, meaning obese.
16. According to the passage, how many Americans are predicted to develop dementia each year by 2060?
A. About half a million
B. About one million
C. About two million
D. About forty-two percent
17. Where were the results of the new study on dementia published?
A. In a popular American newspaper
B. In a book about aging
C. In the publication Nature Medicine
D. In a report by the government
18. At what age is the risk of developing dementia the highest?
A. Before age 55
B. Between age 55 and 65
C. After age 75
D. Only after age 85
19. What is the chance of developing dementia after age 55 if a person lives long enough?
A. Up to 42 percent
B. Exactly 50 percent
C. About 75 percent
D. Less than 10 percent
20. Which of the following is mentioned in the passage as a way to lower the risk of dementia?
A. Getting more sleep every night
B. Controlling high blood pressure
C. Eating less meat
D. Exercising every day
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Researchers recently reported new evidence from a study of a medical device placed inside the body, a spine-stimulating implant. Three people with a muscle-destroying disease got a little stronger after using the implant. They were able to stand and walk more easily because of electrical stimulation to their spinal cord. Marco Capogrosso is an assistant professor at the University of Pittsburg, or Pitt, who led the research. "These people were definitely not expecting an improvement," he said. Capogrosso said, "They were getting better and better," over the study that lasted one month. Returning muscle function The implant was able to return some muscle function, at least temporarily. The implant was already being tested to treat paralysis in other patients. This new evidence suggests it might also aid diseases of the nervous system like spinal muscle atrophy, or SMA.
16. According to the passage, how did the patients benefit from using the implant?
A. They became completely cured of their disease.
B. They got stronger and could stand and walk more easily.
C. They were able to run immediately without pain.
D. They felt no changes in their muscles.
17. Who led the research on this medical device?
A. A patient with spinal muscle atrophy
B. An assistant professor at the University of Pittsburgh
C. A group of paralysis doctors
D. A student from Pitt University
18. How long did the study on the three patients last?
A. One week
B. One month
C. Three months
D. One year
19. What does the passage say about the implant before this study?
A. It was already being tested to treat paralysis.
B. It had never been placed inside a human body.
C. It was only used for patients with spinal muscle atrophy (SMA).
D. It was a brand new device that was not tested before.
20. According to the passage, what might the implant also help with in the future?
A. Preventing people from getting paralysis
B. Diseases of the nervous system like SMA
C. Making healthy people run faster
D. Helping patients recover from heart diseases
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

If you are a gardener, you probably have some favorite plants that you grow year after year. But you might also like to try new plants sometimes. Gardening expert Jessica Damiano recently reported for the Associated Press about one way to find new plants that might appeal to growers. Each year, the independent, nonprofit organization All-America Selections (AAS) enlists more than 80 horticultural professionals from around the United States and Canada to serve as plant-trial judges. Their job is to grow the new plants side-by-side with currently available varieties. The group then compares plant performance, time to reach full growth, flower or fruit size, disease resistance and other qualities important to home gardeners. When the results are gathered, the best plants are named AAS winners for the following year in time for their launch on the market.
16. What do many gardeners like to do besides growing their favorite plants every year?
A. Sell their plants to local markets
B. Try growing new kinds of plants
C. Travel to different countries to find seeds
D. Stop gardening during the winter season
17. Who is Jessica Damiano?
A. A professional gardener who won an AAS award
B. The president of the All-America Selections organization
C. A gardening expert who wrote about finding new plants
D. A plant judge from the United States and Canada
18. How many judges does the All-America Selections organization use?
A. Exactly 50 judges
B. More than 80 judges
C. Less than 10 judges
D. About 100 judges
19. How do the judges test the new plants?
A. They grow them next to plants that are already available.
B. They only study them in high-tech laboratories.
C. They ask home gardeners to grow them first.
D. They buy them directly from local food markets.
20. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a quality that the judges compare?
A. The size of the flowers or fruits
B. The price of the seeds in the market
C. The plant's ability to resist diseases
D. The time it takes for the plant to grow fully
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Air quality differs from place to place and day to day. But about 99 percent of the world’s population breathes air at some point that does not meet pollution guidelines set by the World Health Organization (WHO). Different forms of air pollution kill about 7 million people each year, the U.N. health agency estimates. But health experts say there are some actions individuals can take to protect themselves, even in the world’s most polluted cities. The first step is to start with the understanding that air is not only polluted when it looks smoky or smoggy, said Tanushree Ganguly. She is an air quality expert with the Energy Policy Institute of Chicago’s India program. “Blue skies can’t guarantee you clean air,” Ganguly told The Associated Press. Pollutants and their sources Air pollutants often come from people burning things.
16. According to the passage, what percentage of the world's population breathes air that is not clean enough at some point?
A. 7 percent
B. 50 percent
C. 99 percent
D. 9 percent
17. How many people does the U.N. health agency estimate die each year from air pollution?
A. About 7 million
B. About 99 million
C. About 17 million
D. About 70 million
18. What does Tanushree Ganguly say about blue skies?
A. They always mean the air is clean.
B. They cannot guarantee that the air is clean.
C. They are only seen in Chicago and India.
D. They are the main cause of air pollution.
19. What is Tanushree Ganguly's job?
A. She is a news reporter.
B. She is a health doctor.
C. She is an air quality expert.
D. She is a city leader.
20. According to the passage, what is a common source of air pollutants?
A. Blue skies in large cities
B. Guidelines from health agencies
C. People burning things
D. Windy weather from place to place
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Many of us might only seek out physical therapists if we are recovering from accidental injuries or surgery. However, medical experts say these specialists can also help prevent many health problems if they are seen on a regular basis. One leader in the industry, Sharon Dunn, even calls physical therapists “the best-kept secret in health care.” Dunn is the past president of the American Physical Therapy Association (APTA). She recently told The Associated Press she thinks people need to look at physical therapists in a new way – as health practitioners who can help identify possible medical issues before they appear. Other health experts share this opinion. They include Roger Herr, the current president of the APTA, and Gammon Earhart of Washington University in St. Louis. They both shared Dunn’s prevention message in separate discussions with the AP.
16. According to the passage, when do most people usually go to see a physical therapist?
A. When they want to stay healthy on a daily basis.
B. When they are recovering from injuries or surgery.
C. When they need to get a regular medical check-up.
D. When they are looking for a new health secret.
17. How often should people visit physical therapists to help prevent health problems?
A. On a regular basis.
B. Only after an accident.
C. Once a year.
D. Whenever they feel pain.
18. What term does Sharon Dunn use to describe physical therapists?
A. The most expensive health workers
B. The best-kept secret in health care
C. The leaders of modern medicine
D. The only practitioners for surgery
19. According to Sharon Dunn, what is a new way people should look at physical therapists?
A. As specialists who only treat serious accidents
B. As health practitioners who can find medical issues before they appear
C. As medical experts who work mainly with the media
D. As assistants to help other doctors in hospitals
20. Who is Roger Herr?
A. The past president of the APTA
B. A reporter for the Associated Press
C. The current president of the APTA
D. A professor at Washington University
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Art Deco is an international artistic style that was popular in the 1920s and 1930s. Now, interest in the style and its qualities is growing. A new generation is enjoying the style’s roots and turning it into something new. A public showing, or exhibition, at the Museum of the City of New York provides a look at the style that helped define the city 100 years ago. Famous Art Deco buildings include the Chrysler Building, the Empire State Building, Rockefeller Center and Radio City Music Hall. The public showing, called “Art Deco City: New York Postcards from the Leonard A. Lauder Collection,” also has clothing and film clips from the time. In London, the Victoria and Albert Museum has a collection of day and evening clothing, jewelry and more. The collection comes from Les Ballets Russes, a dance company that had a major effect on fashion.
16. When was the Art Deco style first popular?
A. In the 1800s
B. In the 1920s and 1930s
C. In the 1950s and 1960s
D. Only in the last ten years
17. According to the passage, what is the new generation doing with the Art Deco style?
A. They are changing it into something new.
B. They are ignoring its historical roots.
C. They are using it only to design buildings.
D. They are trying to stop its popularity.
18. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the text as a famous Art Deco building?
A. The Chrysler Building
B. The Empire State Building
C. The Central Park Tower
D. Radio City Music Hall
19. Besides postcards, what else can visitors see at the "Art Deco City" exhibition in New York?
A. Jewelry and modern paintings
B. Clothing and film clips
C. Musical instruments and books
D. Large stone statues
20. Where did the collection of clothing and jewelry at London's Victoria and Albert Museum come from?
A. A famous film studio
B. The Museum of the City of New York
C. A dance company
D. The Leonard A. Lauder Collection
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

This Sunday, February 9, is not a holiday in the United States, but it might seem like one to Americans. Many will be attending parties to watch Super Bowl LIX. The Super Bowl is the championship game of professional American football. The National Football League, or NFL, has 32 teams mainly based in large cities across the U.S. The first professional league was formed in 1920, when representatives of four teams met in Canton, Ohio. The group first called itself the American Professional Football Association but changed the name two years later to the National Football League. In 1960, Texas businessman Lamar Hunt pushed the effort to create the American Football League, or AFL. The two leagues competed with each other to sign top football players from universities around the country.
16. According to the passage, what is the Super Bowl?
A. A official national holiday in the United States.
B. The championship game of professional American football.
C. A meeting of university football representatives.
D. A sports league based in Canton, Ohio.
17. How many teams currently make up the National Football League (NFL)?
A. 4 teams
B. 9 teams
C. 32 teams
D. 60 teams
18. Where was the first professional football league formed in 1920?
A. Canton, Ohio
B. Texas
C. New York
D. Washington
19. What was the National Football League's original name?
A. The American Football League
B. The American Professional Football Association
C. The Canton Ohio Association
D. The Lamar Hunt League
20. Who pushed the effort to create the American Football League (AFL) in 1960?
A. Representatives of four teams in Ohio
B. Top university football players
C. A Texas businessman named Lamar Hunt
D. The original founders of the NFL
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

A new museum of laughter in Croatia is offering to help people deal with the negativity of modern life. Visitors to the HaHaHouse in the Croatian capital Zagreb walk into a little cloud of white smoke when they step inside. Its purpose is to blow away their worries before they climb into a "giant washing machine". Then they move down a twisting slide into a space filled with little white balls where their trip to a happier place starts. Forty-three-year-old Andrea Golubic is the museum’s creator. Golubic said she got the idea during the COVID-19 pandemic. At that time, many people were feeling down, depressed and alone: "I realized that I had a mission -- to heal people with laughter." The idea "came straight from the heart", Golubic told the French News Agency (AFP).
16. Where is the HaHaHouse museum located?
A. In France
B. In Zagreb, Croatia
C. In a cloud of smoke
D. In a giant washing machine
17. What is the purpose of the white smoke that visitors walk into first?
A. To wash their dirty clothes
B. To help them forget their worries
C. To make them look like balls
D. To show them how to laugh
18. What do visitors slide down into after the "giant washing machine"?
A. A pool of warm water
B. An outdoor playground
C. A space filled with small white balls
D. A dark, quiet room
19. When did Andrea Golubic get the idea to create the museum?
A. During the COVID-19 pandemic
B. When she was a 43-year-old child
C. While traveling around France
D. After visiting a washing machine factory
20. According to Golubic, how did the idea for the museum come to her?
A. From a news report
B. From an old book about laughter
C. From a hospital doctor
D. Straight from her heart
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Buddhist nuns in Nepal are once again performing martial arts for the public after a five-year break caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. The women recently put on a show for onlookers at their religious community, called a monastery, outside the capital Kathmandu. The performance was attended by reporters from Reuters news agency. Members of the hilltop Druk Amitabha Monastery are part of the 1,000-year-old Drukpa religious organization. The group gives nuns equal status to male community members called monks. The Drukpa lineage includes the only female order in the male-led Buddhist monastery system. The nun community was established in 2009 and now has 300 members between the ages of six and 54. The nuns come from Bhutan, India and Nepal. They are all trained in kung fu, the ancient Chinese style martial art that promotes self-defense and strength.
16. Why did the nuns stop performing kung fu for the public for five years?
A. Because of the COVID-19 pandemic.
B. Because they moved to a new monastery.
C. Because they did not have enough members.
D. Because they wanted to focus on studying.
17. What makes the Drukpa religious organization special compared to other Buddhist systems?
A. It is managed entirely by children.
B. It gives equal status to both nuns and monks.
C. It does not allow members to practice martial arts.
D. It is located in the center of Kathmandu.
18. In which year was the nun community established?
19. The nuns in the community come from which countries?
A. China, Nepal, and Bhutan
B. India, Nepal, and China
C. Bhutan, India, and Nepal
D. Nepal, Bhutan, and Vietnam
20. According to the passage, kung fu is practiced by the nuns to promote what?
A. Winning international competitions
B. Self-defense and strength
C. Entertainment for foreign tourists
D. Traditional Nepalese dance
Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

There is a growing movement worldwide to use plants in building design to establish closer connections with nature and soften hard city styles. The movement also seeks to improve designs to help protect the environment and increase personal wellness. Many of the efforts by architects and designers have centered on one natural element: trees. Examples of such design can be found in many places, including New York City. A building in Manhattan that houses the Ford Foundation has a 12-story light-filled entrance area, or atrium. It is also filled with plants and flowers including magnolias, eucalyptus, jacaranda, cryptomeria, iron bark and pear trees. Another example sits within New York City’s Brookfield Place shopping center. The center’s main atrium includes a collection of 12-meter-tall Washingtonia palm trees. In Singapore, the city-state’s Jewel Changi airport offers travelers an inviting environment.
16. What is one goal of the growing worldwide movement mentioned in the passage?
A. To make buildings look taller than before
B. To establish closer connections with nature
C. To replace city buildings with public parks
D. To reduce the cost of modern building designs
17. According to the passage, what specific natural element have architects and designers focused on the most?
A. Water
B. Rocks
C. Trees
D. Sunlight
18. How tall is the entrance area (atrium) of the Ford Foundation building in Manhattan?
A. 12 stories
B. 12 meters
C. 12 feet
D. 12 yards
19. Where can visitors find the Washingtonia palm trees in New York City?
A. At the Jewel Changi airport
B. Inside the Ford Foundation building
C. At the Brookfield Place shopping center
D. In a park in the middle of Manhattan
20. What does the passage say about Singapore's Jewel Changi airport?
A. It is the largest airport in Asia.
B. It offers a welcoming environment for travelers.
C. It features the tallest trees in the world.
D. It is located near Manhattan.
